questions.prompt: The probability of rolling a 6 on a fair die is:
questions.explanation: A fair die has 6 equally likely outcomes; P(6) = 1/6.

questions.prompt: For two independent events A and B, P(A ∩ B) = ?
questions.explanation: Independence means P(A ∩ B) = P(A) × P(B).

questions.prompt: The mean of a normal distribution N(μ, σ²) is:
questions.explanation: For N(μ, σ²), the mean (expected value) is μ and the variance is σ².

questions.prompt: The mean of the dataset {2, 4, 6, 8, 10} is:
The standard deviation σ is related to variance σ² by:
questions.explanation: Standard deviation is the positive square root of variance: σ = √(Var(X)).

questions.prompt: In a standard normal distribution, what percentage of data falls within ±1σ?
Bayes' theorem states that P(A|B) = ?
questions.explanation: Bayes' theorem: P(A|B) = P(B|A)P(A)/P(B). Updates prior P(A) with likelihood P(B|A).

questions.prompt: A binomial distribution B(n, p) has mean E[X] = ?
questions.explanation: E[X] = np; Var(X) = np(1−p) for B(n, p).

questions.prompt: In a Poisson distribution with rate λ, E[X] = Var(X) = ?
questions.explanation: For Poisson(λ): both mean and variance equal λ.

questions.prompt: The median of {3, 7, 2, 9, 5} (after sorting) is:
questions.explanation: Sorted: {2, 3, 5, 7, 9}. Middle (3rd) value = 5.

questions.prompt: The probability P(A ∪ B) (A union B) for non-mutually-exclusive events is:
questions.explanation: Inclusion-exclusion: P(A ∪ B) = P(A) + P(B) − P(A ∩ B) to avoid double-counting the intersection.

questions.prompt: The coefficient of variation (CV) is defined as:
questions.explanation: CV = σ/μ × 100%. It normalizes variability by the mean, useful for comparing dispersions.

questions.prompt: In hypothesis testing, a Type I error is:
choices: A. Accepting H₀ when it is false
B. Rejecting H₀ when it is true
C. Failing to reject H₀ when it is false
questions.explanation: Type I error (α): rejecting the null hypothesis when it is actually true (false positive).

questions.prompt: The exponential distribution with rate λ has mean:
questions.explanation: For Exp(λ): E[X] = 1/λ, Var(X) = 1/λ². Used for time-between-events in Poisson processes.

questions.prompt: In reliability engineering, the reliability R(t) of a component is related to the CDF F(t) by:
questions.explanation: R(t) = P(T > t) = 1 − F(t), where F(t) is the probability of failure before time t.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are measures of central tendency? (Select ALL that apply.)
questions.explanation: Mean, median, and mode measure central tendency. Variance measures dispersion (spread).

questions.prompt: The Central Limit Theorem states that the sampling distribution of the sample mean approaches:
choices: A. A uniform distribution as n → ∞
B. A normal distribution as n → ∞, regardless of the population distribution
questions.explanation: CLT: for large n, x̄ ~ N(μ, σ²/n) regardless of the shape of the underlying population.

questions.prompt: The variance of {1, 2, 3, 4, 5} is (use population variance):
questions.explanation: Mean = 3; deviations²: 4,1,0,1,4; sum = 10; σ² = 10/5 = 2.

questions.prompt: Linear regression line y = mx + b: the slope m is estimated by:
questions.explanation: Least-squares slope m = Σ[(x−x̄)(y−ȳ)] / Σ[(x−x̄)²] = S_{xy}/S_{xx}.

questions.prompt: A 95% confidence interval for the mean means:
choices: A. 95% of individual values fall in the interval
B. The probability that μ is in the interval is exactly 95%
C. If repeated samples are taken, about 95% of such intervals will contain the true mean
D. The sample mean is within 95% of the population mean
questions.explanation: A CI is a procedural statement: 95% of confidence intervals constructed this way will contain the true parameter.

questions.prompt: P(A|B) reads as 'the probability of A _____ B'.
questions.explanation: P(A|B) = 'probability of A given B' — conditional probability that A occurs given we know B has occurred.

questions.prompt: The hazard rate h(t) in reliability is defined as:
questions.explanation: Hazard rate h(t) = f(t)/R(t) = instantaneous failure rate given survival to time t.

questions.prompt: Two fair coins are flipped. What is the probability (as a fraction, decimal) of getting exactly one head?
questions.explanation: Sample space: {HH, HT, TH, TT}. One head: {HT, TH} = 2 outcomes. P = 2/4 = 0.5.

questions.prompt: The correlation coefficient r ranges between:
questions.explanation: Pearson correlation r ∈ [−1, 1]; r = 1 perfect positive, r = −1 perfect negative, r = 0 uncorrelated.

questions.prompt: MTTF (Mean Time to Failure) for an exponential distribution with failure rate λ is:
questions.explanation: MTTF = E[T] = 1/λ for exponentially distributed failure times (memoryless property).
